stem:
Daniel rode a stationary bike for forty minutes. His speed during the ride is summarized as follows:

  • From minute 0 to minute 10, his speed increased steadily from 0 mph to 12 mph.
  • From minute 10 to minute 20, his speed remained constant at 12 mph.
  • From minute 20 to minute 35, his speed decreased steadily from 12 mph to 4 mph.
  • From minute 35 to minute 40, his speed increased steadily from 4 mph to 14 mph.

According to this description, which of the following statements is NOT true concerning Daniel's ride?

answerOptions.content: Daniel rode at a constant speed for ten minutes.
Daniel's speed decreased at a constant rate during a fifteen-minute period.
Daniel's maximum speed occurred during the last five minutes of his ride.
Daniel's speed was increasing for a longer period of time than it was decreasing.

rationale:
Choice D is correct (the NOT-true statement). Daniel's speed was increasing for 10 + 5 = 15 minutes total but decreasing for 15 minutes — they are equal, so it is not true that increasing took longer.

Choices A, B, and C are all true based on the description.
